description: Unified eval suite: coding and tool-calling. References pre-generated by Claude Opus into references.json.

function.name: create_appointment
function.description: Schedule a new appointment for a client

service_type.description: Service type: swedish_massage, deep_tissue, hot_stone, sports_massage, facial

appointment_date.description: Date of the appointment to cancel in YYYY-MM-DD format

function.name: reschedule_appointment
function.description: Move an existing appointment to a new date and time

original_date.description: Current date of the appointment in YYYY-MM-DD format

new_date.description: New date for the appointment in YYYY-MM-DD format

new_time.description: New start time in HH:MM 24-hour format

function.name: check_availability
function.description: Check whether a specific time slot is available for a new booking

date.description: Date to check in YYYY-MM-DD format

time.description: Start time to check in HH:MM 24-hour format

function.name: get_client_appointments
function.description: Retrieve existing appointments for a specific client

date_from.description: Start of date range in YYYY-MM-DD format (optional, defaults to today)

date_to.description: End of date range in YYYY-MM-DD format (optional)

coding_prompts.prompt: Write a Python function that implements a trie data structure with insert, search, and prefix-match methods. Include type hints and handle edge cases.

Write a bash script that monitors a directory for new CSV files and loads them into a SQLite database. The script should: detect new files using inotifywait, infer column names from CSV headers, create tables if they don't exist, and handle errors gracefully.

Implement an async rate limiter in Python using asyncio that supports: token bucket algorithm, configurable rate and burst size, and a decorator for rate-limiting async functions. Include proper error handling.

tool_call_prompts.system_prompt: You are a scheduling assistant for a wellness center. Today's date is 2026-03-11 (Wednesday). Use the available tools to help with appointment scheduling. If information required to complete a request is missing, ask for it rather than guessing.
tool_call_prompts.user_message: Book Sarah Chen for a 60-minute deep tissue massage on March 15th at 2:00 PM.
tool_call_prompts.notes: Baseline: all args provided explicitly. Tests basic tool invocation and argument mapping.

tool_call_prompts.system_prompt: You are a scheduling assistant for a wellness center. Today's date is 2026-03-11 (Wednesday). Use the available tools to help with appointment scheduling. If information required to complete a request is missing, ask for it rather than guessing.
tool_call_prompts.user_message: Please book Marcus Webb for tomorrow morning at 9 — something for his sore upper back, half an hour.
tool_call_prompts.notes: Natural language parsing: relative date (tomorrow=2026-03-12), duration (half an hour=30). Service inference from symptom description — deep_tissue or sports_massage are both valid. Judge should accept either.

tool_call_prompts.system_prompt: You are a scheduling assistant for a wellness center. Today's date is 2026-03-11 (Wednesday). Use the available tools to help with appointment scheduling. If information required to complete a request is missing, ask for it rather than guessing.
tool_call_prompts.user_message: Schedule a hot rock session for Jennifer Kim, 90 minutes, March 22nd at 1:00 PM.
tool_call_prompts.notes: Service name normalization: 'hot rock session' → hot_stone. All other args explicit.

tool_call_prompts.system_prompt: You are a scheduling assistant for a wellness center. Today's date is 2026-03-11 (Wednesday). Use the available tools to help with appointment scheduling. If information required to complete a request is missing, ask for it rather than guessing.
tool_call_prompts.user_message: Please cancel Priya Sharma's appointment on March 18th — she texted saying she'll be traveling for work.
tool_call_prompts.notes: Tool selection: cancel not create. Model must ignore irrelevant narrative detail (travel reason is optional context, not a required arg).

tool_call_prompts.system_prompt: You are a scheduling assistant for a wellness center. Today's date is 2026-03-11 (Wednesday). Use the available tools to help with appointment scheduling. If information required to complete a request is missing, ask for it rather than guessing.
tool_call_prompts.user_message: Move Jennifer Kim's March 22nd appointment to the 29th at the same time — 1:00 PM.
tool_call_prompts.notes: All args present. Must use reschedule_appointment — not cancel+create. Tests whether the model understands the purpose of the reschedule tool vs. decomposing into multiple actions.

tool_call_prompts.system_prompt: You are a scheduling assistant for a wellness center. Today's date is 2026-03-11 (Wednesday). Use the available tools to help with appointment scheduling. If information required to complete a request is missing, ask for it rather than guessing.
tool_call_prompts.user_message: Can you reschedule Marcus Webb's appointment to next week?
tool_call_prompts.notes: Missing: which appointment (original_date) and new time. Model must NOT call reschedule_appointment with guessed/fabricated args — must ask for the missing details.

tool_call_prompts.system_prompt: You are a scheduling assistant for a wellness center. Today's date is 2026-03-11 (Wednesday). Use the available tools to help with appointment scheduling. If information required to complete a request is missing, ask for it rather than guessing.
tool_call_prompts.user_message: Is there an opening for a 60-minute session this Friday around 3pm?
tool_call_prompts.notes: Natural date resolution required: 'this Friday' = 2026-03-13. No explicit date provided. Tests check_availability selection over create_appointment (no client name given — not a booking request).

tool_call_prompts.system_prompt: You are a scheduling assistant for a wellness center. Today's date is 2026-03-11 (Wednesday). Use the available tools to help with appointment scheduling. If information required to complete a request is missing, ask for it rather than guessing.
tool_call_prompts.user_message: What does Sarah Chen have booked for the rest of this week?
tool_call_prompts.notes: Tests get_client_appointments. 'Rest of this week' implies date_from=today, date_to=2026-03-15 (Sunday) — judge evaluates whether date range is sensible, but only client_name is required.

tool_call_prompts.system_prompt: You are a scheduling assistant for a wellness center. Today's date is 2026-03-11 (Wednesday). Use the available tools to help with appointment scheduling. If information required to complete a request is missing, ask for it rather than guessing.
tool_call_prompts.user_message: Is Jennifer Kim coming in on the 22nd?
tool_call_prompts.notes: Hard semantic trap. 'Is [client] coming in on [date]' = look up existing appointment, NOT check slot availability. check_availability asks whether a slot is free for new bookings — wrong tool here. Tests whether the model understands tool semantics vs. surface pattern matching.

tool_call_prompts.system_prompt: You are a scheduling assistant for a wellness center. Today's date is 2026-03-11 (Wednesday). Use the available tools to help with appointment scheduling. If information required to complete a request is missing, ask for it rather than guessing.
tool_call_prompts.user_message: Marcus Webb wants to know if he can get in for a Swedish massage this Saturday at 10am for an hour.
tool_call_prompts.notes: Intent is to check availability, not book. 'Wants to know if he can get in' ≠ confirmed booking. Model must not call create_appointment despite having all booking args. 'This Saturday' = 2026-03-14.

tool_call_prompts.system_prompt: You are a scheduling assistant for a wellness center. Today's date is 2026-03-11 (Wednesday). Use the available tools to help with appointment scheduling. Answer general questions from your own knowledge without using tools.
tool_call_prompts.user_message: What's the difference between Swedish massage and deep tissue?
tool_call_prompts.notes: Model should NOT call any tool. Tests restraint — answering a general knowledge question when no scheduling action is needed.

tool_call_prompts.system_prompt: You are a scheduling assistant for a wellness center. Today's date is 2026-03-11 (Wednesday). Use the available tools to help with appointment scheduling. If information required to complete a request is missing, ask for it rather than guessing.
tool_call_prompts.user_message: I'd like to book an appointment.
tool_call_prompts.notes: Maximally underspecified booking request. Model must NOT call create_appointment with fabricated args — must ask for client name, service, date, and time.

tool_call_prompts.system_prompt: You are a scheduling assistant for a wellness center. Today's date is 2026-03-11 (Wednesday). Use the available tools to help with appointment scheduling. If you don't have the information needed to answer a question, say so clearly.
tool_call_prompts.user_message: What's your late cancellation fee if I cancel within 24 hours?
tool_call_prompts.notes: Policy question outside the model's knowledge and outside the available tools. Model must NOT fabricate a fee — should acknowledge it doesn't have that information and direct the client to check with staff.
